howItWorks.intro: This regular polygon area calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is a regular polygon?
faq.answer: A regular polygon has all sides equal in length and all interior angles equal in measure. Examples include equilateral triangles, squares, regular pentagons, and regular hexagons.

faq.question: What is the apothem of a polygon?
faq.answer: The apothem is the distance from the center of a regular polygon to the midpoint of any side. It is perpendicular to that side and is used in the area formula: A = ½ × perimeter × apothem.

faq.question: How do you find the interior angle of a regular polygon?
faq.answer: The interior angle of a regular polygon with n sides is (n-2) × 180° / n. For example, a regular hexagon has interior angles of (6-2) × 180° / 6 = 120°.

faq.question: What is the sum of exterior angles of any polygon?
faq.answer: The sum of exterior angles of any convex polygon is always 360°, regardless of the number of sides. Each exterior angle of a regular polygon equals 360°/n.
